recommended_paths: approve strategic direction with lazy-load, package-location, and mandatory web gate test constraints
exact_next_step: Record reviewer resolutions in the governing TODO using the machine-checkable resolution table or equivalent gate ledger, then extract the derived resolution packet and decide whether another bounded review pass is still required.

findings.title: Web anonymous promotion gate must be a mandatory pre-merge gate
findings.rationale: The favorite/app-promotion behavior was the critical regression. The proposal lists Playwright evidence but must make it mandatory before merge, including no phone login and no auto-open app before explicit CTA.
findings.suggested_action: Elevate Playwright coverage for web anonymous promotion gate to a non-negotiable implementation gate.

findings.title: Shared package ownership needs clarification
findings.rationale: The proposal names TenantPublicActionSheet/BellugaActionSheet but not its package location. Putting it under invite or promotion would create ownership/circular dependency risk.
findings.suggested_action: Place canonical action sheet primitives under shared presentation, e.g. lib/presentation/shared/action_sheets/, with feature-owned content builders.

findings.title: Lazy-load boundary is not yet formalized
findings.rationale: The proposal asks whether first-touch should avoid eager hydration but does not make it a requirement. Without that, implementation might fetch inviteable recipients, sent statuses, or contacts on sheet open.
findings.suggested_action: Add a hard requirement: first-touch action sheet shall render from static event/promotion data and defer invite recipient/status/contact hydration until full composer or explicit action.

findings.title: Convidar modal vs share immediate is an accepted tradeoff
findings.rationale: Two hero actions are immediate and one opens a sheet. This is justified by invite domain state, but should be explicit in the contract.
findings.suggested_action: Document the semantic split between multi-path invite and single-intent share launchers.
